title: ConvertKit vs GetResponse (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit and GetResponse. See pricing, features, and which email marketing platform wins for creators and course sellers.
h1: ConvertKit vs GetResponse: Complete Comparison for 2025
intro: Both ConvertKit and GetResponse are popular email marketing platforms designed for creators, but they take different approaches. ConvertKit focuses on simplicity for bloggers and digital product creators, while GetResponse offers a more comprehensive all-in-one marketing suite. This guide breaks down the key differences to help you choose the right platform for your needs.

comparison_table.tool_a: Visual automations, commerce features, clean interface

choose_a_if: You prioritize a simple, creator-friendly interface over advanced features
You need strong e-commerce and product sales capabilities built-in
You want a generous free tier with up to 1,000 subscribers at no cost
choose_b_if: You need webinar hosting and online course functionality included
You want the lowest starting price and more comprehensive marketing tools
You're building complex conversion funnels and need automation depth

faq.a: Neither is universally 'better'—it depends on your needs. ConvertKit excels for creators who want simplicity and built-in commerce features, while GetResponse is better for those needing webinars, courses, and advanced automation. GetResponse offers more features at a lower price, but ConvertKit has a more intuitive interface and higher user satisfaction on G2.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or GetResponse?
faq.a: GetResponse is cheaper, starting at $19/month compared to ConvertKit's $29/month. GetResponse also offers a larger free tier (500 subscribers) than ConvertKit (1,000 subscribers). However, ConvertKit's pricing may represent better value if you primarily need email marketing and e-commerce features.

faq.q: Can I switch from GetResponse to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, you can export your email list from GetResponse and import it into ConvertKit. Both platforms support CSV exports, making migration straightforward. However, automation sequences, landing pages, and other created content will need to be recreated manually in your new platform.
